title: wtf-actor: Define SupervisionEvent enum and supervisor message handler

resolved_clarifications.question: What is the source of SupervisionEvent?
resolved_clarifications.answer: SupervisionEvent comes from ractor framework as part of its actor supervision system

resolved_clarifications.question: What is BoxedState in ActorTerminated?
resolved_clarifications.answer: ractor's BoxedState is a type-erased state storage; we use Option<BoxedState> to allow actors to optionally return their final state on termination

assumptions.assumption: SupervisionEvent variants will be extended via ractor's SupervisionEvent
assumptions.validation_method: Check ractor 0.15 source for SupervisionEvent definition

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL handle all SupervisionEvent variants from ractor
THE SYSTEM SHALL log all supervision events for debugging

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL increment running child count and log the event
THE SYSTEM SHALL remove child from registry and decrement running count
THE SYSTEM SHALL evaluate restart policy and decide whether to restart

unwanted.condition: IF unknown SupervisionEvent variant is received
unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT panic or ignore the event silently
unwanted.because: Silent ignoring hides bugs; panicking crashes the supervisor

required_inputs.constraints: Must be valid actor reference to the supervisor itself
Must be one of the defined variants
Must contain children HashMap and running_count

return_guarantees.guarantee: Ok(()) on successful handling, Err(ActorProcessingErr) on failure

side_effects: Tracing logs emitted for each event type

invariants: children count matches running_count for started/terminated actors
Supervisor actor never terminates while children are running (unless shutdown policy)

files_to_read.what_to_extract: SupervisionEvent handling patterns and restart policy examples
SupervisionEvent flow and registry management patterns

patterns_to_find.purpose: Understand error handling in actor context

external_docs.extract: Exact enum definition with all variants

research_questions.question: What are all variants of ractor's SupervisionEvent?
How does ActorCell relate to ActorRef?

research_complete_when: All research_requirements files have been read

data_integrity_failures.failure: Child removed from registry but running_count not decremented
data_integrity_failures.prevention: Use atomic operations or verify invariant after each event

integration_failures.failure: Supervisor handles event before child is fully registered

happy_paths.name: test_actor_started_increments_count
happy_paths.given: Supervisor with 2 running children receives ActorStarted
then: Child cell added to children registry

edge_cases.scenario: Actor terminates but returns no state
edge_cases.expected: Handler completes without error, child removed

contract_tests.name: test_invariant_running_count_never_negative
contract_tests.test: Terminate children repeatedly, verify count never goes negative

pipeline_test.name: test_supervision_event_pipeline
pipeline_test.description: Spawn linked child, verify started event, kill child, verify terminated event

gate_0_research.name: Research Gate
checks: All research_requirements files have been read
evidence_required: Research notes with file contents summarized

gate_1_tests.name: Test Gate
checks: Tests fail with expected 'not implemented' errors

tasks.task: Read ADR-003 and ADR-006 for supervision patterns

tasks.what: Write unit tests for handle_supervisor_evt function covering ActorStarted, ActorTerminated, ActorFailed cases
tasks.done_when: Test exists and compiles (fails on unimplemented)

tasks.task: Implement SupervisionEvent enum extension if needed
tasks.what: Define SupervisionEvent wrapper enum if ractor's needs extension, or use ractor's directly

tasks.depends_on: Implement SupervisionEvent enum extension if needed
tasks.what: Implement supervisor event handler matching on all event variants with proper state updates

failure_modes.likely_cause: Duplicate termination events handled multiple times

where_to_look.what_to_check: Check if termination removes child before decrementing

failure_modes.fix_pattern: Use saturating_sub or check existence before decrement

tests: All acceptance tests written and passing
All error path tests written and passing

related_files.relevance: Actor model decisions and SupervisionEvent usage
Two-level hierarchy with MasterOrchestrator and WorkflowInstance

do: Use ractor's SupervisionEvent directly - don't reimplement it
Use saturating arithmetic for counters to prevent underflow
Log all supervision events with tracing::info! or tracing::error!
do_not: Do NOT reimplement ractor's SupervisionEvent - use from ractor
Do NOT use unwrap() on Option types
Do NOT assume child exists before removing - check first

title: wtf-actor: Define RestartPolicy enum and SupervisorConfig struct

resolved_clarifications.question: What restart policies are needed?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Always (restart unconditionally), Never (never restart), OnError { max_retries: u32 } (restart up to N times)

resolved_clarifications.question: What fields belong in SupervisorConfig?
resolved_clarifications.answer: max_children: usize, restart_policy: RestartPolicy, restart_delay_ms: u64

assumptions.assumption: RestartPolicy will be stored per-supervisor, not per-child

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide RestartPolicy with Always, Never, OnError variants
THE SYSTEM SHALL provide SupervisorConfig with max_children, restart_policy, restart_delay_ms

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL track retry count and compare against max_retries

unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT restart the child (0 retries means no restart)
unwanted.because: Semantic confusion - 0 retries should mean no retries, not infinite

return_guarantees.guarantee: Always means restart on any failure, Never means no restart, OnError means restart up to max_retries
All fields accessible via public accessors

prior_art.what_to_learn: Permanent, transient, temporary strategies map to Always, OnError, Never

research_questions.question: How does Erlang OTP handle restart strategies?

research_complete_when: Erlang OTP restart strategies understood and mapped to our enum

contract_tests.name: test_invariant_max_children_positive
contract_tests.test: Create SupervisorConfig, verify max_children is always > 0

pipeline_test.name: test_restart_policy_pipeline
pipeline_test.description: Test all restart policies in isolation

tasks.what: Write unit tests for RestartPolicy::should_restart method covering all variants
Write unit tests for SupervisorConfig default and accessors
Define RestartPolicy enum with Always, Never, OnError { max_retries: u32 } variants and should_restart(self, retry_count: u32) -> bool method
Define SupervisorConfig struct with max_children, restart_policy, restart_delay_ms fields and Default implementation

failure_modes.symptom: OnError with max_retries=0 causes infinite loop
failure_modes.likely_cause: Logic error treating 0 as 'unlimited' instead of 'none'
failure_modes.fix_pattern: OnError with 0 should return false immediately

apis_that_do_not_exist: RestartPolicy struct - this is what we are creating
SupervisorConfig struct - this is what we are creating

related_files.relevance: Restart policy patterns from Erlang supervision

do: Use exhaustive match on RestartPolicy variants
Implement Default for SupervisorConfig with sensible defaults
Make RestartPolicy fields public or provide accessor methods
do_not: Do NOT implement RestartPolicy::default() - policies are not default-constructible
Do NOT use magic numbers for default values

title: wtf-actor: Implement restart_child() with retry tracking and delay

resolved_clarifications.question: How is restart delay implemented?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Using tokio::time::sleep with restart_delay_ms from config

resolved_clarifications.question: How is retry count tracked?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Per-child retry count stored in a HashMap<ActorCell, u32>

assumptions.assumption: Children can be restarted by re-spawning with same name

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL track retry count per child actor
THE SYSTEM SHALL apply restart delay before restarting

event_driven.trigger: WHEN restart_child is called with child cell
event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL check restart policy, increment retry count, and respawn if allowed

unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT restart the child under any circumstances
THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT restart the child

required_inputs.constraints: Must be a valid child of this supervisor

system_state: Child must exist in children registry

state_changes: SupervisionEvent::ActorStarted will fire on successful restart

return_guarantees.guarantee: Ok(()) if restart initiated, Err(SupervisorError) if restart not allowed

side_effects: New actor spawned with same name as original

invariants: retry_counts never exceeds what restart policy allows
Child is removed from children only on ActorTerminated, not on restart

usability_failures.failure: Restart delay causes parent to appear unresponsive
usability_failures.prevention: Make delay configurable, default to 1 second

happy_paths.name: test_restart_child_succeeds_with_always_policy
happy_paths.given: Supervisor with RestartPolicy::Always and child in registry

happy_paths.name: test_restart_child_succeeds_within_retry_limit
happy_paths.given: Supervisor with OnError { max_retries: 3 } and retry_count 2

error_paths.name: test_restart_child_fails_at_retry_limit
error_paths.given: Supervisor with OnError { max_retries: 3 } and retry_count 3

tasks.what: Write unit tests for restart_child covering success and failure cases
Implement restart_child that checks policy, updates retry count, applies delay, and respawns child actor

where_to_look.what_to_check: Ensure async context and proper await

failure_modes.fix_pattern: Make restart_child async and await sleep

do: Track retry count per child in HashMap
do_not: Do NOT use std::thread::sleep - must be async
Do NOT restart without checking policy first

title: wtf-actor: Implement stop_all_children() and get_children() supervisor methods

resolved_clarifications.question: How is stop_all_children implemented in ractor?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Using Actor::kill or sending a poison pill message

resolved_clarifications.question: What is return type of get_children?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Vec<ActorCell> containing all child actor cells

assumptions.assumption: stop_all_children sends termination signal to all children

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide get_children returning all child actor cells
THE SYSTEM SHALL provide stop_all_children terminating all children

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL send termination signal to each child actor

unwanted.condition: IF stop_all_children is called while children are restarting
unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT leave children in zombie state

state_changes: children HashMap cleared after termination events are processed

return_guarantees.guarantee: Returns Vec of all child ActorCells at moment of call
All children receive termination; returns when all confirmed terminated

side_effects: Termination signals sent to all children

invariants: After stop_all_children completes, children HashMap should be empty

patterns_to_find.purpose: Find ractor's method for stopping actors

happy_paths.name: test_stop_all_children_terminates_all
then: children HashMap empty after events processed

contract_tests.name: test_invariant_no_duplicate_children
contract_tests.test: Call get_children multiple times, verify no duplicates

tasks.what: Write unit tests for get_children and stop_all_children
Implement get_children() -> Vec<ActorCell> that returns copy of children keys
Implement stop_all_children() that iterates children and sends termination signal

failure_modes.likely_cause: Children don't respond to termination signal

do: get_children should return a snapshot (Vec) not a reference
stop_all_children should handle case of empty children gracefully
do_not: Do NOT return mutable reference to children HashMap
Do NOT block indefinitely if child doesn't respond

title: wtf-actor: Implement escalation handling and death notification to parent

resolved_clarifications.question: When does escalation happen?
resolved_clarifications.answer: When child fails with error that cannot be handled at supervisor level (max retries exceeded, or critical error)

resolved_clarifications.question: How is death notification sent to parent?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Using ractor's linked supervision - when supervisor can't restart child, it itself fails and notifies its parent

assumptions.assumption: Escalation happens via linked actor failure propagation

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL propagate unhandled failures to parent supervisor via linked supervision

event_driven.trigger: WHEN child restart fails or max retries exceeded
event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL record death notification and optionally escalate to parent

unwanted.condition: IF supervisor cannot restart child and has no parent
unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT swallow the error silently
unwanted.because: Error is lost; no indication of failure

required_inputs.constraints: Must be the child that failed
The error that caused the failure

return_guarantees.guarantee: Ok(()) if escalation handled, Err if no parent to escalate to

side_effects: Supervisor's own failure may be triggered if it cannot handle

invariants: If escalation fails and no parent exists, error is logged but not swallowed

integration_failures.failure: Supervisor crashes due to unhandled child failure
integration_failures.prevention: Handle ActorFailed events properly, implement restart or clean death

happy_paths.name: test_escalation_logs_death_notification
then: Death notification logged with error details

tasks.what: Write unit tests for escalation and death notification
Implement escalation logic in handle_supervisor_evt when restart fails - log death notification and propagate via linked supervision

failure_modes.symptom: Error swallowed silently on escalation failure
failure_modes.likely_cause: No logging or error handling when parent notification fails

where_to_look.what_to_check: Ensure all error paths are logged

failure_modes.fix_pattern: Always log errors, even if escalation to parent fails

related_files.relevance: Supervision event handling and escalation patterns

do: Let linked supervision handle parent notification automatically
do_not: Do NOT manually propagate - ractor handles linked propagation
